steps.title: Match the layout explanation
steps.content: The researchers explain that rearranging a store makes shoppers search through more areas, exposing them to more products and increasing unplanned purchases. A supporting response must include both parts of that process: a planned item was not in its usual place, and the shopper bought something extra while searching.

steps.title: Reject different purchase causes
steps.content: Choice B shows searching but no extra purchase. Choice C attributes the purchase to a coupon, which is an obvious marketing cue rather than a layout effect. Choice D says the shopper chose a larger store for planned items, not that a rearranged layout caused a spontaneous purchase. Choice A shows relocated cleaning supplies leading the shopper to notice and buy a notebook. The answer is A.
